pullRequest.title: DOM-75511 Add appInfo to app search results

nodes.body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_

<details>
<summary>🧩 Analysis chain</summary>

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
#!/bin/bash
# Read-only verification: locate Joda format strings using week-year token.
rg -n 'toString\(".*YYYY' --type scala
```

Repository: cerebrotech/domino

Length of output: 280

---

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
# Get context around the two YYYY occurrences
sed -n '250,260p' server/app/domino/server/search/Results.scala
echo "---"
sed -n '311,321p' server/app/domino/server/search/Results.scala
```

Repository: cerebrotech/domino

Length of output: 994

---

🌐 Web query:

`Joda-Time YYYY vs yyyy formatting calendar year week-year difference`

💡 Result:

In **Joda‑Time pattern strings**:

- `yyyy` = **calendar year** (can be negative for BCE, depending on chronology).  
- `YYYY` = **year of era** (≥ 0). For AD/CE dates it usually *looks the same as* `yyyy`, but it differs for BCE/era handling.  
- **Week-year is *not* `Y` in Joda‑Time**: it is `x` / `xxxx` (**weekyear**) and is meant to be used with `w` (**week of weekyear**). For ISO week dates you’d typically use something like `xxxx-'W'ww-e`. [1]

**Calendar year vs week-year (why they can differ):** ISO weeks are “owned” by a **weekyear**; week 1 is the week that contains the **first Thursday** of the calendar year, so the first ISO week can start in late December of the previous calendar year, and the last ISO week can extend into early January of the next calendar year. That’s why a late‑December date can format as next year when using weekyear (`xxxx`). [2]

**Rule of thumb:**  
- If you want the “normal” year for dates like `2026-12-31`, use `yyyy`.  
- If you’re producing ISO week-based dates (year + week number), use `xxxx` with `ww`.  

**Sources:**  
[1] Joda‑Time “Formatting” (pattern letters: `y` year, `Y` year-of-era, `x` weekyear, `w` week-of-weekyear) https://www.joda.org/joda-time/key_format.html  
[2] Joda‑Time “ISO8601 calendar system” (weekyear definition and boundary behavior) https://www.joda.org/joda-time/cal_iso.html

---



</details>

**Use `yyyy` (calendar year) instead of `YYYY` (year of era) in created timestamp formatting.**

In Joda-Time, `YYYY` is the year-of-era token and differs from `yyyy` (calendar year) when formatting non-AD chronologies. For standard timestamp formatting, `yyyy` is the conventional choice. This issue also appears at line 255.

<details>
<summary>Suggested fixes</summary>

```diff
-        created.toString("MMM d, YYYY @ h:mma"),
+        created.toString("MMM d, yyyy @ h:mma"),
```

```diff
-        created.map(_.toString("MMM d, YYYY @ h:mma")).getOrElse(""),
+        created.map(_.toString("MMM d, yyyy @ h:mma")).getOrElse(""),
```
</details>

<!-- suggestion_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Committable suggestion</summary>

> ‼️ **IMPORTANT**
> Carefully review the code before committing. Ensure that it accurately replaces the highlighted code, contains no missing lines, and has no issues with indentation. Thoroughly test & benchmark the code to ensure it meets the requirements.

```suggestion
        created.map(_.toString("MMM d, yyyy @ h:mma")).getOrElse(""),
```

</details>

<!-- suggestion_end -->

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In `@server/app/domino/server/search/Results.scala` at line 316, The timestamp
formatting uses the Joda pattern "MMM d, YYYY @ h:mma" which should use calendar
year token "yyyy"; update the format string passed to
created.map(_.toString(...)) (and the other occurrence noted around line 255) to
"MMM d, yyyy @ h:mma" so the created mapping uses the correct year token.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:poseidon:hawk -->
